lit: Whom does my soul tremble for?
sm: Whom does my soul fear?

syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ψυχή μου. Verb: τρέμει. Interrogative object: τίνα.
syn.rhet: Parallel to the preceding lamenting questions, expressing fear and uncertainty.

src: τίς μοι σκοτεινὸς περικέχυται ὅλῳ τῷ σώματι;
tok: has been poured around; has enveloped
lit: What dark thing has enveloped my whole body?
sm: What dark thing has spread over my whole body?

syn.svo: Subject: τίς σκοτεινός. Verb: περικέχυται. Dative of reference: μοι. Dative phrase: ὅλῳ τῷ σώματι.
syn.rhet: Personifies darkness as a surrounding force.

lit: If [you are] father, help [me],
sm: If you are a father, help me,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a comma because the conditional series continues.

syn.sub: One member of a four-part conditional series that continues through line 167.
syn.svo: Protasis subject/predicate: πατήρ. Apodosis verb: βοήθησον. Object is implied.
syn.rhet: First member of the rapid family/friend/enemy sequence.

lit: But if [you are] mother, have compassion [on me],
sm: if you are a mother, have compassion on me,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a comma because the conditional series continues.

syn.sub: Second member of the same series.
syn.svo: Protasis subject/predicate: μήτηρ. Apodosis verb: συμπάθησον. Object is implied.
syn.rhet: The appeal shifts from help to compassion.

lit: But if [you are] a brother, speak to [me],
sm: if you are a brother, speak to me,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a comma because the conditional series continues.

syn.sub: Third member of the same series.
syn.svo: Protasis subject/predicate: ἀδελφός. Apodosis verb: προσλάλησον. Object is implied.
syn.rhet: The plea narrows to intimate speech.

lit: But if [you are] a friend, stand with [me]
sm: if you are a friend, stand with me,

syn.sub: Fourth member of the same series; the sentence continues in line 167.
syn.svo: Protasis subject/predicate: φίλος. Apodosis verb: συστάθητι. Object is implied.
syn.rhet: The sequence escalates from help to companionship.

src: εἰ δὲ ἐχθρός, ἀπαλλάγηθι, ὅτι πρωτότοκος ἐγώ.
lit: But if [you are] an enemy, depart, because I am firstborn.
sm: if you are an enemy, depart, because I am a firstborn.

syn.mc: Conditional protasis with imperative apodosis and causal clause.
syn.sub: ὅτι πρωτότοκος ἐγώ explains the imperative ἀπαλλάγηθι.
syn.svo: Protasis subject/predicate: ἐχθρός. Apodosis verb: ἀπαλλάγηθι. Causal clause subject: ἐγώ. Predicate: πρωτότοκος.
syn.rhet: The list culminates in exclusion: enemy, depart.

src: πρὸ δὲ τοῦ σιωπῆσαι τὸν πρωτότοκον
lit: And before the firstborn fell silent
sm: and before the firstborn fell silent

syn.sub: πρὸ τοῦ + infinitive marks temporal precedence.
syn.svo: Infinitival subject/object: τὸν πρωτότοκον. Infinitive: σιωπῆσαι.
syn.rhet: Signals the moment just before silence seizes the firstborn.

src: ἡ μακρὰ σιωπὴ κατέσχεν αὐτὸν προσειποῦσα"
ana: ἡ μακρὰ σιωπὴ κατέσχεν αὐτὸν προσειποῦσα.
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg f
lit: The long silence held him, addressing him.
sm: The long silence held him, speaking to him.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a period.

syn.sub: The participle προσειποῦσα modifies ἡ μακρὰ σιωπή.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ μακρὰ σιωπή. Verb: κατέσχεν. Object: αὐτόν. Participial modifier: προσειποῦσα.
syn.ptc: προσειποῦσα personifies silence as speaking while holding the firstborn.
syn.rhet: Powerful personification: silence becomes an agent of address.

lit: You are my firstborn
sm: You are my firstborn.

syn.mc: Direct speech; verbless in effect except for εἶ.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: εἶ. Predicate nominative: Πρωτότοκος ἐμὸς.
syn.rhet: A brief identity statement answered by the next line.

src: ἐγώ σοι πέπρωμαι ἡ τοῦ θανάτου σιωπή.
lit: I have been allotted to you, the silence of death.
sm: I am destined for you as the silence of death.

syn.mc: Main declarative clause in direct speech.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: πέπρωμαι. Dative of advantage/recipient: σοι. Predicate/apposition: ἡ τοῦ θανάτου σιωπή.
syn.app: ἡ τοῦ θανάτου σιωπή apposes or further identifies the speaking self.
syn.rhet: The personified silence identifies itself as death's silence.

src: ἕτερος δέ τις πρωτότοκος νοήσας τὴν τῶν πρωτοτόκων ἅλωσιν
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: But another firstborn, having perceived the seizure of the firstborns,
sm: but another firstborn, after perceiving the seizure of the firstborns,

syn.mc: Participial subject phrase introducing the next firstborn's response.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause in this line; the finite verb follows in line 174.
syn.svo: Subject: ἕτερος δέ τις πρωτότοκος. Participle: νοήσας. Object: τὴν ... ἅλωσιν.
syn.ptc: νοήσας is antecedent action, explaining the later self-denial.
syn.rhet: Shifts from direct speech to another firstborn's reaction.

src: ἑαυτὸν ἀπαρνεῖτο ἵνα μὴ θάνῃ πικρῶς"
ana: ἑαυτὸν ἀπαρνεῖτο ἵνα μὴ θάνῃ πικρῶς.
lit: He was denying himself in order not to die bitterly.
sm: He denied himself so that he might not die bitterly.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a period.

syn.sub: ἵνα μὴ θάνῃ πικρῶς expresses purpose or avoidance.
syn.svo: Subject: the other firstborn. Verb: ἀπαρνεῖτο. Object: ἑαυτόν.
syn.ptc: The preceding line's νοήσας sets up this imperfective reaction.
syn.rhet: Self-denial becomes a desperate strategy for survival.

lit: I am not firstborn,
sm: I am not a firstborn,
emd: Corrected εἶμι -> εἰμί; the context requires 'am', not 'go'.

syn.mc: Direct speech, self-identification in the negative.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: εἰμί. Predicate adjective: πρωτότοκος, negated by οὐκ.
syn.rhet: The speaker tries to evade the firstborn's fate by self-definition.

lit: I have been born in a third fruit.
sm: I was born third.
emd: <UNCERTAIN: τριτῷ γεγέννημαι καρπῷ is idiomatic and semantically obscure; the sense is likely 'born third'.>

syn.svo: Subject: implied ἐγώ. Verb: γεγέννημαι. Dative phrase: τριτῷ καρπῷ.
syn.rhet: The speaker attempts to disqualify himself by ordinal status.

src: ὁ δὲ ψευσθῆναι μὴ δυνάμενος τοῦ πρωτοτόκου προσήπτετο,
tok: pres mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: But the one unable to lie was clinging to the firstborn,
sm: But the one unable to be false clung to the firstborn,

syn.sub: ψευσθῆναι μὴ δυνάμενος is an infinitival/participial descriptive phrase qualifying the subject.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ... προσήπτετο. Verb: προσήπτετο. Object: τοῦ πρωτοτόκου. Participial/infinitival modifier: μὴ δυνάμενος ψευσθῆναι.
syn.ptc: μὴ δυνάμενος frames the subject's inability as the reason for clinging.
syn.rhet: The scene turns from self-denial to physical attachment.

tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And he was falling prone in silence.
sm: and he fell prone in silence.

syn.svo: Subject: implicit firstborn. Verb: ἔπιπτεν. Predicative adjective: πρηνής. Participial modifier: σιγῶν.
syn.ptc: σιγῶν gives the manner of the fall, reinforcing mute resignation.
syn.rhet: Silence and bodily collapse are fused.

src: ὑπὸ δὲ μίαν ῥοπὴν" ὁ πρωτότοκος καρπὸς τῶν Αἰγυπτίων ἀπώλετο
ana: ὑπὸ δὲ μίαν ῥοπὴν ὁ πρωτότοκος καρπὸς τῶν Αἰγυπτίων ἀπώλετο.
lit: And in a single sweep the firstborn fruit of the Egyptians was destroyed.
sm: And in a single instant the firstborn fruit of the Egyptians was destroyed.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a period.

syn.mc: Main clause concluding the prior scene.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ πρωτότοκος καρπὸς τῶν Αἰγυπτίων. Verb: ἀπώλετο. Adverbial phrase: ὑπὸ δὲ μίαν ῥοπὴν.
syn.rhet: The mortality of the Egyptian firstborn is cast as sudden and total.

lit: the first-sown one,
sm: the first-born,

syn.svo: Apposition to the destroyed firstborn fruit of the Egyptians.
syn.rhet: Begins a fourfold appositional list of titles.

lit: the firstborn,
sm: the firstborn,
emd: Followed change_log.txt pattern: corrected ὃ -> ὁ as article.

syn.svo: Continues the appositional list naming the firstborn Egyptian child.
syn.rhet: Parallel title within the list.

lit: the beloved one,
sm: the beloved one,
emd: Followed change_log.txt pattern: corrected ὃ -> ὁ as article.

syn.svo: Continuation of the appositional list.
syn.rhet: The series intensifies the pathos by naming the cherished child.

tok: was thrown down; was dashed to the ground
lit: the dearly cherished one was thrown down to the ground.
sm: the dearly cherished one was dashed to the ground.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ἠδαφίσθη -> ἐδαφίσθη. Trailing OCR quotation mark removed.

syn.mc: Main clause completing the appositional list.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ περίψυκτος. Verb: ἐδαφίσθη. Adverb: χαμαί.
syn.rhet: Climactic collapse of the Egyptian firstborn into the earth.

lit: not that of humans only,
sm: not only that of humans,
emd: Followed change_log.txt pattern: corrected ὃ -> ὁ as article.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the previous clause.
syn.svo: Depends on the understood noun 'loss' or 'death' from line 183.
syn.rhet: Expands the scope of the destruction beyond humanity.

lit: but also of irrational animals.
sm: but also of irrational animals.

syn.mc: Completes the correlative correction begun in line 184.
syn.svo: Genitive phrase depending on the understood scope of the previous loss.
syn.rhet: The lament is widened to the animal world.

src: μύκημα δὲ ἐν τοῖς πεδίοις τῆς γῆς ἠκούετο
lit: And a moaning was heard in the fields of the land
sm: and a moaning was heard in the fields of the land,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 187 adds a genitive-participial expansion.
syn.svo: Subject: μύκημα. Verb: ἠκούετο. Locative phrase: ἐν τοῖς πεδίοις τῆς γῆς.
syn.rhet: Sound imagery broadens the scene from the court to the countryside.

src: ἀποδυρομένων κτηνῶν ἐπὶ τῶν τροφίμων αὐτῶν"
ana: ἀποδυρομένων κτηνῶν ἐπὶ τῶν τροφίμων αὐτῶν.
tok: pres mid ptcp gen pl n/m
lit: of beasts lamenting over their young.
sm: of beasts lamenting over their young.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark removed; punctuation normalized to a period.

syn.mc: Genitive absolute / genitive participial phrase dependent on line 186.
syn.sub: The participle phrase explains the source of the moaning.
syn.svo: Participle: ἀποδυρομένων. Subject of the participial clause: κτηνῶν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῶν τροφίμων αὐτῶν.
syn.ptc: ἀποδυρομένων forms a genitive absolute with the beasts as lamenting agents.
syn.rhet: The suffering is now described as maternal and animal lament.

lit: For even the heifer with calf
sm: for even the heifer with calf,

syn.mc: Paratactic nominal clause continuing the lament scene.
syn.sub: No finite verb is expressed in this line; the phrase prepares the continuation in lines 189-190.
syn.svo: Subject: δάμαλις. Predicate adjective: ὑπόμοσχος.
syn.rhet: Adds a concrete example of animal suffering.

lit: and the horse with foal
sm: and the horse with foal,

syn.mc: Paratactic nominal clause continuing the same list.
syn.svo: Subject: ἵππος. Predicate adjective: ὑπόπωλος.
syn.rhet: The animal lament extends from cattle to horses.

src: καὶ τὰ λοιπὰ κτήνη λοχευόμενα καὶ σπαργῶντα
tok: pres mp ptcp nom pl n
pres act ptcp nom pl n
lit: and the remaining beasts being in labor and thrashing
sm: and the rest of the beasts, laboring and writhing,

syn.mc: Participial nominal phrase continuing the animal lament list.
syn.sub: No finite verb in this line; the sentence continues beyond the batch boundary.
syn.svo: Subject: τὰ λοιπὰ κτήνη. Participles: λοχευόμενα and σπαργῶντα.
syn.ptc: Both participles are circumstantial/descriptive and intensify the scene of animal distress.
syn.rhet: The catalogue widens from individual beasts to the whole remainder of the herd.
